text:
IMPARTIAL NEWS + INTELLIGENT DEBATE

Account

It was a fabulous Friday for Team GB as the medals continue to rack up

PARIS —Team GBsurged up theParis 2024medal table with their most successful day yet in the French capital.

Emily Craig and Imogen Grant earnedthe first of three goldson a fantastic Friday by completing their revenge mission in the lightweight double sculls.

The pair missed out on a medal by one-hundredth of a second in Tokyo – a photo finish Craig has had pinned to her wall ever since.

She will now be able to replace it with a happier image as the duo finished comfortably clear of Romania and Greece to continue their remarkable unbeaten run in this cycle.

“Tokyo was part of our story and this Olympics was the grand finale,” Grant said.

“Not every Olympian gets it right on its first try and we didn’t back in Tokyo. But we have put in so much work and we are much better people this time around.”

Bryony Page continued the British momentum with a landmark gold in women’s trampolining.

The 33-year-old, who won silver in Rio and bronze in Tokyo, completed the set in style to become Team GB’s first ever Olympic champion in the sport.

She reiterated a long-held desire to perform as part ofCirque du Soleilin the aftermath of her triumph but this is not the end of her Olympic journey.

“I’ve got more I want to do on trampoline so I won’t be going straight away,” she said.

“I want to show what my glass ceiling is and break through it.”

There was more history created against the fitting backdrop of Versailles as Harry Charles followed his father Peter on to the Olympic podium.

Charles, 25, joined Scott Brash and Ben Maher to guide Team GB to glory in team showjumping – an event he watched from the sidelines as a 13-year-old atLondon 2012when his dad won gold.

In doing so, they are the first father-son combination to win Olympic gold for Team GB since 1948 – rowers Dickie and Charles Burnell (1908) the last to do it.

But Harry Charles must surely be the first to have won alongside his dad’s teammates, the triumphant quartet in London being Maher, Brash, Peter Charles and Nick Skelton, in a slightly different format.

Harry said: “They are both my heroes, guys I’ve looked up to since I started riding. To be on the podium with them, my inner, younger self is freaking out a little bit.”

Four more medals were banked either on or in the water. The men’s pair could not quite match the women’s success as rowers Ollie Wynne-Griffith and Tom George, who led from almost start to finish, were edged out by Croatia’s Martin and Valent Sinkovic.

Ben Proud broke his Olympic duck at the third attempt with a thrilling silver in the men’s 50m freestyle whileDuncan Scottfinished runner-up to face of the GamesLeon Marchandin the men’s 200m individual medley, the Frenchman claiming a remarkable fourth gold.

Jack Laugher and Anthony Harding, meanwhile, had to master the toughest dive in the world to win an Olympic bronze medal in the 3m synchro.

It is a fourth Olympic medal for Laugher and Harding’s first on debut.

On the first day of athletics, Josh Kerr, Keely Hodgkinson andDina Asher-Smithwere among those to ease through their opening rounds but Morgan Lake failed to make the high jump final.

“I’m still not quite sure what went on,” she said. “I was maybe thinking ahead to Sunday too much, focusing on the final before I even got through qualifying.”

There was British disappointment, meanwhile, in the BMX racing. Defending champion Beth Shriever cruised through the heats but got squeezed out early on in the final and came home eighth, while Kyle Whyte – who won silver in Tokyo – was taken to hospital after a crash in his semi-final.

Watch every moment of Paris 2024 live only on discovery+, the streaming home of the Olympics.

title: Medal rush continues as Team GB win gold in trampolining, showjumping and rowing
author: Sportsbeat
description: It was a fabulous Friday for Team GB as the medals continue to rack up
